title: campanula rotundifolia subsp. scheuchzeri
intro: Campanula rotundifolia subsp. scheuchzeri, also known as Alpine Harebell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Alpine Harebell include its ability to reduce coughs and relieve congestion, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce stress levels. Alpine Harebell can be prepared for consumption as a tea or infusion, or in the form of a tincture, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for its medicinal benefits.
